# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.
# Category: path_traversal

class FilesController < ApplicationController
  def download
    uploads_dir = Rails.root.join('uploads')

    base_uploads_resolved_path = uploads_dir.realpath rescue nil

    unless base_uploads_resolved_path
      render file: "#{Rails.root}/public/404.html", status: :not_found, layout: false
      return
    end

    requested_filename = params[:filename].to_s
    potential_file_path = Pathname.new(File.join(uploads_dir, requested_filename)).cleanpath

    resolved_file_path = potential_file_path.realpath rescue nil

    unless resolved_file_path &&
           File.file?(resolved_file_path) &&
           resolved_file_path.to_s.starts_with?(base_uploads_resolved_path.to_s + File::SEPARATOR)

      render file: "#{Rails.root}/public/404.html", status: :not_found, layout: false
      return
    end

    send_file resolved_file_path,
              filename: File.basename(resolved_file_path),
              disposition: 'attachment'
  rescue ActionController::MissingFile
    render file: "#{Rails.root}/public/404.html", status: :not_found, layout: false
  end
end
